items.title: Juru-Sungai Dua traffic project remains on track, hits 28.75 pct progress
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Juru-Sungai Dua Traffic Dispersal Project (PTJSD) continues to record positive progress, with Package 1 of the project achieving 28.75 per cent overall completion as of July 12 and remaining on schedule. PLUS Malaysia Berhad (PLUS) said major milestones achieved include the completion of preliminary works, while utility relocation works have reached 70 per cent and geotechnical works have recorded 68 per cent progress. PLUS said that upon completion in October 2027, PTJSD would help improve traffic flow, reduce congestion, enhance safety and provide a more comfortable travel experience for highway users and local residents.

items.title: MACC seeks two Bangladeshi witnesses in corruption trial
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) is seeking two Bangladeshi men to testify as prosecution witnesses in an ongoing corruption trial at the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court. MACC said efforts to locate the first witness, Fardin Mahi, at his last known address at Menara City One, Jalan Munshi Abdullah, Kuala Lumpur, were unsuccessful. Another prosecution witness, Md Sumon Hossain, also could not be traced at his last known address at Residensi Pandanmas, Kampung Pandan, Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Why has the Iran-US ceasefire memorandum frayed?
items.summary: The 14-point Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) declared an end to the war and the Strait of Hormuz open for commercial vessels. On Monday, he said the United States would probably take over the Strait of Hormuz. The US has said the strait will remain toll-free.

items.title: Negri Sembilan polls: PN to unveil candidates on Thursday
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Perikatan Nasional will announce its Negri Sembilan election candidates on Thursday, a day after Barisan Nasional unveils its line-up. PN information chief Tan Sri Annuar Musa said the timing would provide a clearer picture of the coalition's electoral strategy ahead of nomination day. A day later, on Thursday, PN will announce its list of candidates.

items.title: Forum seeks stronger animal welfare framework, regional cooperation
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia is stepping up efforts to strengthen its animal welfare framework through greater regional cooperation, policy development and the adoption of science-based practices at the Animal Welfare Forum 2026 held here. The Department of Veterinary Services (DVS) said the forum was organised by its Veterinary Regulatory Division in strategic collaboration with A World of Good Initiative Inc. "Among the key topics discussed are effective animal welfare governance, the implementation of animal welfare strategies in the Asia-Pacific region, the One Welfare concept, community well-being, livestock welfare challenges, poultry welfare and aquaculture welfare," it said in a statement today. The forum aligns with the Malaysian Animal Welfare Strategic Plan 2021-2030, which aims to enhance governance, compliance and implementation of animal welfare practices through a strategic approach based on science, innovation and collaboration.

items.title: Jailed 'Geng Upik' leader charged with RM800,000 money laundering
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: A convicted member of the organised crime group 'Geng Upik' was charged in the Sessions Court here today with nine counts of money laundering involving more than RM800,000. The accused, Datuk Maslan Sani, 42, pleaded not guilty before Judge Zaini Fishir @ Fisal. Maslan was charged with receiving proceeds of unlawful activities amounting to RM5,500, RM20,000, RM175,090, RM33,990, RM4,170, RM29,900, RM284,400, RM139,700 and RM129,200 through deposits into two of his bank accounts.

items.title: Melaka govt allocates RM200,000 to empower cooperatives this year, says exco
items.summary: State Entrepreneur Development, Cooperatives and Consumer Affairs Committee chairman Allex Seah Shoo Chin said grants of up to RM20,000 are available for multi-cluster cooperatives, while newly established cooperatives can receive up to RM50,000. "The scope of assistance includes grants of up to RM10,000 to finance business development costs, such as the purchase of equipment, machinery and new business fittings, including computerised automation systems. Seah said the state government also provides grants of up to RM10,000 to finance working capital for business operations or additional capital to expand business activities.

items.title: A-G–public prosecutor split Bill deferred pending Rulers' briefing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The constitutional amendment to separate the roles of the Attorney-General and public prosecutor will be deferred to the next Dewan Rakyat sitting pending a briefing with the Conference of Rulers. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the briefing would include an additional amendment aimed at strengthening Parliament's role in the appointment process. "The government intends to proceed with the second and third readings of the Constitutional (Amendment) (No.2) Bill 2026, including debate and decision-making, at the next Dewan Rakyat sitting after the Conference of Rulers has been briefed on the proposed amendment," she told the Dewan Rakyat.

items.title: ‘Give us the time under the mandate,’ Anwar says as pressure mounts for early GE16 after Johor polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today brushed aside calls for an early general election, urging rivals to allow his administration to complete its full term to safeguard economic growth and political stability. He said voters wanted the government to focus on running the country rather than being drawn into continuous political campaigning. The current Parliament’s term can run until February 2028 unless dissolved earlier, but speculation over an early general election has grown following political strains within the unity government.

items.title: Malaysia pushes for stronger Asean role in refugee crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is seeking stronger burden-sharing among Asean member countries to address the regional refugee crisis, alongside closer cooperation with international migration and humanitarian organisations. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said regional efforts should also focus on achieving a political solution that would allow Rohingya refugees to return home voluntarily, safely and with dignity. He said Malaysia would continue strengthening cooperation with Asean and the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) through a more strategic and comprehensive approach.

items.title: Foodie Media posts RM4.15mil earnings in Q3
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Foodie Media Bhd recorded a net profit of RM4.15 million in the third quarter ended May 31, 2026 (Q3 FY26), reflecting lower one-off initial public offering listing expenses. For the nine-month period, Foodie Media recorded revenue of RM39.7 million and a net profit of RM11 million. Foodie Media had RM49.5 million in cash and short-term deposits, with total borrowings of RM1.1 million comprising hire purchase and lease liabilities.

items.title: Witness Protection Programme to be strengthened with wider access, stronger safeguards
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is seeking to expand access to the Witness Protection Programme, improve participants' welfare, and extend protections to former participants even after they leave the scheme. Presenting the Witness Protection (Amendment) Bill for its second reading in the Dewan Rakyat, Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the proposed amendments were built on four main objectives. They are to expand access to the programme, strengthen its governance and administration, improve participants' welfare and well-being, and extend protection to former participants after they leave the programme.

items.title: Expert witness in Zara inquest is not a registered child clinical psychologist
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The Coroner's Court was told that the 76th witness in the Zara Qairina Mahathir inquest - Dr Noor Aishah Rosli - was not a registered child clinical psychologist under the Malaysian Allied Health Professions Council (MAHPC). Noor Aishah, however, told the court she had two qualifications as registered counsellor and child clinical psychologist. Nahra: Doctor, you are not a child clinical psychologist registered under that Act?

items.title: iCaur V23: Dares to be different
items.summary: In a market where most electric SUVs chase sleek aerodynamics and futuristic styling, the icaur V23 goes in the opposite direction. Launched in Malaysia at RM119,800 for the rear-wheel-drive variant and RM132,800 for the all-wheel-drive iWD, the V23 occupies an increasingly competitive price band. Yet rather than competing on outright performance or range, it sells a lifestyle proposition - one that blends urban practicality with adventure-inspired styling.

items.title: Thai bandmates recount chaos of deadly Bangkok bar fire [WATCH]
items.summary: BANGKOK: First the band saw smoke. The inferno left at least 28 people dead, including two members of the cover band on Sunday night's bill. At least two other bandmates were injured, among around 70 people who suffered from burns, smoke inhalation and other injuries, authorities said.

items.title: New national housing policy to set affordable home prices using local income data
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — The government plans to use local income and housing demand data to set affordable home prices under the National Housing Policy 2026-2035. Deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Datuk Aiman Athirah Sabu said the ministry was finalising the policy as part of efforts to address rising prices and mismatches between housing supply and demand. Aiman Athirah said the ministry had mapped affordable housing prices using state- and district-level median household income data from the Household Income and Basic Amenities Survey 2024.

items.title: New Malaysian passport wins praise at home and abroad
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The enhanced Malaysian passport has received positive feedback from the public and overseas immigration authorities since its launch earlier this month, says Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban. He said the new passport had drawn praise for its attractive design, faster issuance process and enhanced security features. "Immigration authorities abroad were also impressed with the new passport design," he told reporters today.

items.title: Complexity remorse in the electrification transition - A strategic view
items.summary: THERE is a specific kind of buyer's remorse that will start building quietly in Malaysia's car market and I think the building blocks are caused by misframing of the current technology. New energy vehicles - cars running partly or fully on batteries rather than petrol alone - will likely cross 15 per cent of total industry volume in Malaysia by the end of 2026, although there is a likelihood this may happen slightly earlier. By the numbers, Malaysia is no longer an early-adopter NEV market.

items.title: Male infertility 'no longer overlooked' under BuAI programme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government, through the National Population and Family Development Board (LPPKN), is providing special subsidies for B40 and M40 couples struggling to conceive. The assistance is provided under the Fertility Treatment Assistance and Infertility Advocacy (BuAI) programme, said LPPKN chairman Datuk Seri Rohani Abdul Karim. "So the government, through the National Population and Family Development Board, gives special subsidies for B40 and M40 couples," she said.

items.title: Pas open to cooperation, but focus remains on PN for Negri polls
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Pas remains open to political cooperation but is focused on strengthening Perikatan Nasional (PN) ahead of the Negri Sembilan election. Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said the party had always been open to discussions, but any cooperation would depend on both sides reaching a mutually beneficial outcome. Yesterday, Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the party had no agreement with Pas on seat negotiations for the Negri Sembilan election.

items.title: Zeekr expands dealer network with three new partners
items.summary: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Malaysia (Zeekr Malaysia) is expanding its authorised dealer network as it seeks to strengthen its retail and aftersales presence nationwide amid growing demand for premium electric vehicles. The company has appointed three new dealer partners - Quantum Motors Sdn Bhd, Wheelcorp Premium Sdn Bhd and Ghee Hua Co Sdn Bhd - to broaden its footprint in Peninsular Malaysia, Sabah and Sarawak. The latest appointments will increase Zeekr Malaysia's retail presence to 11 outlets nationwide.

items.title: Invest Selangor launches 'BLAST' to integrate SMEs into global supply chain
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Invest Selangor Bhd has launched the Invest Selangor Business Linkages & Advancement Programme (BLAST), to assist small and medium enterprises (SMEs) integrate into high value global supply chains. The programme, launched by Selangor executive councillor for investment, trade and mobility Ng Sze Han, seeks to connect local SMEs with multinational corporations (MNCs), mid tier companies (MTCs) and industry anchor companies to improve their competitiveness and market readiness. BLAST is part of the Second Selangor Plan (RS-2) under the Local Market Integration Programme and builds on the state's industrial development efforts under the First Selangor Plan.

items.title: New era for Gibraltar as border controls with Spain set to end
items.summary: MADRID: Thousands of workers crossing daily between Spain and Gibraltar will enter a new era of easier travel on Wednesday, as border checks that have long been a source of tension are lifted. During rush hours, long lines can form at the land border and documents are checked – especially during periods of tension between Britain and Spain, which claims sovereignty over Gibraltar. But under an agreement reached between Brussels and London following Britain's exit from the European Union, border controls between Gibraltar and Spain will be eliminated from July 15.

items.title: Housing Ministry says 1,615 troubled housing projects worth RM150.86b rehabilitated since 2022
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — The government rehabilitated 1,615 delayed, sick and abandoned housing projects involving 190,422 units with a gross development value of RM150.86 billion between December 2022 and May 2026. Deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Datuk Aiman Athirah Sabu said the achievement was part of the ministry’s efforts to address stalled private housing projects through a task force established in December 2022. Aiman Athirah said the Ministry of Housing and Local Government acted as a mediator between homebuyers and developers to help revive problematic projects.

items.title: Malaysia–Thailand border zone to open up access to Indochina markets, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — The development of the Malaysia–Thailand Border Economic Zone (BEZ) will provide Malaysian goods with wider access to the markets of Laos, Cambodia and Vietnam, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said the BEZ aims to unlock broader economic development beyond Sadao and Bukit Kayu Hitam, which were jointly launched with Thailand’s Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul several days ago. Anwar said the border development initiative would prioritise small and medium-sized enterprises, job creation and skills training to ensure local communities benefit from increased economic activity.

items.title: Ex-IGP Musa Hassan must pay RM250,000 to Hannah Yeoh after Federal Court rejects appeal bid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 14 — The Federal Court here today dismissed former Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Musa Hassan’s application for leave to appeal against the Court of Appeal’s ruling, which ordered him to pay RM250,000 in damages to Federal Territories Minister Hannah Yeoh in a defamation suit. The panel also ordered Musa to pay RM50,000 in costs to Hannah. Hannah was present in court today.

items.title: Boy's false kidnap report leads to school bullying case
items.summary: Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the Padang Terap police headquarters initially received a report that the Form Two student had been abducted by two unidentified men in Kuala Nerang on Sunday. Police later found the teenager by the roadside near Kota Putra, Durian Burung, in Padang Terap. Adzli said police later discovered that the kidnapping claim was false.

items.title: Six Pakatan lawmaker demand major rewrite of FOI Bill
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Six Pakatan Harapan lawmakers have called for major changes to the Freedom of Information Bill 2026, describing the government's draft as unsatisfactory and inferior to existing laws in Selangor and Penang. He also urged the government to complete an advanced draft of the Ombudsman Bill and present it together with the FOI Bill to the new Parliamentary Special Select Committee. Yet MPs have not been presented with this Bill," he said.

items.title: UNHCR cardholders not above Malaysian law, says Immigration DG
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) cardholders are not exempt from enforcement action if they commit offences under Malaysian law, despite being allowed to remain in the country on humanitarian grounds, says Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban. He said UNHCR cardholders were exempt only from immigration action for overstaying and not possessing valid immigration documents. "We cannot take action against them for overstaying or not having valid documents if they hold a UNHCR card.

items.title: Khaled: Malaysia will not rely on single power bloc for defence needs
items.summary: Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the country's procurement strategy was guided by national security interests and Malaysia's neutral and non-aligned foreign policy. Khaled said the government would also strengthen the National Defence Industry Policy by promoting technology transfer, developing the domestic defence industry and expanding local maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) capabilities. He said these measures would reduce Malaysia's dependence on foreign suppliers and strengthen the country's defence self-reliance.

items.title: Malaysia's falling fertility rate mirrors 'global trend'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's Total Fertility Rate of 1.6, against a replacement rate of 2.1, reflects a global trend rather than a uniquely Malaysian crisis. United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) assistant representative Tengku Aira Tengku Razif said the phenomenon is observed globally, with South Korea's rate being significantly lower at 0.9. She also dismissed the notion that young people are avoiding marriage or children by choice, citing a global UNFPA survey.

items.title: US lawmakers demand Pentagon release findings from probe of Iran school strike
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Democratic senators led by Kirsten Gillibrand called on Monday for President Donald Trump's administration to disclose within the next week the findings from a US military investigation into a Feb 28 strike at a girls' school in Iran. Reuters first reported on March 5 that an initial, internal US military investigation showed US forces were likely responsible for the fatal strike in Minab on the opening day of the war with Iran. The group of more than two dozen US senators, including the top Democrat on the Senate Armed Services Committee, Jack Reed, requested in a letter the US military finalise its investigation, brief Congress and present a plan to ensure such a mistake does not happen again.

items.title: Malaysia's biggest income group can afford RM200,000-RM300,000 homes
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Malaysia's largest household income group earns between RM3,000 and RM5,000 a month, pointing to an affordable housing price range of about RM200,000 to RM300,000, according to the latest research by Rehda Institute. "Housing for all means fair access. Ng said social and public housing should remain government-led, while supporting the establishment of a single national housing agency to better coordinate housing policies.

items.title: Housing affordability must go beyond purchase price, says Syed Saddiq
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government should expand its definition of housing affordability beyond purchase price alone to include renovation, maintenance and other costs faced by young homebuyers, says Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman. Speaking during the Ministers' Question Time session in the Dewan Rakyat, Syed Saddiq said the current measure was largely based on income levels, with homes priced below RM300,000 generally considered affordable. "Even if an affordable home is available and vacant, there are renovation costs, maintenance costs and additional expenses that need to be taken into account," he said in a supplementary question to Deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Datuk Aiman Athirah Sabu.

items.title: Man pleads not guilty to possessing firearms and 6,158 bullets
items.summary: LUMUT: An unemployed man pleaded not guilty at the Seri Manjung Sessions Court here to three charges of possessing a rifle, five air rifles, and 6,158 bullets. On the first charge, Ling was accused of possessing a 9mm calibre rifle in a wooden hut behind a house in Kampung Cina, Sitiawan, on the night of July 1. For the second charge, the unrepresented accused was charged with possessing five unlicensed air rifles.

items.title: King receives briefing from MACC chief Abdul Halim on commission’s latest developments
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today granted an audience to Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman at Istana Negara. According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar’s official Facebook page, His Majesty was briefed on the latest developments concerning the commission during the 30-minute audience. Abdul Halim, a former High Court judge, assumed office as the new MACC chief commissioner on May 13, succeeding Tan Sri Azam Baki.

items.title: Smoke complaints around Sungai Petani leads Kedah DOE to illegal Bukit Banyan waste dump site
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, July 14 — The Kedah Department of Environment (DOE) has detected an illegal solid waste disposal site involving open burning activities in Bukit Banyan, near here. She said investigations found illegal dumping of domestic waste, or solid waste, on a plot of land that had been cleared of vegetation. “The area covered with domestic waste or solid waste spans about 250 square metres.

items.title: ‘Meets expectations’: Revised AG-PP separation Bill wins backing from previously dissenting PKR MPs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — Several PKR backbenchers who had objected to the original Bill to decouple the public prosecutor’s role from the Attorney General’s Chambers said today they welcomed the revised edition and would back its enactment. Subang MP Wong Chen (PKR) cited the move to allow the House of Representatives to vet candidates for the post of public prosecutor as among the key reasons for their backing of the revised Bill. Wong Chen said he hopes the Bill would be debated alongside the Prime Minister's Term Limit Bill at this sitting.

items.title: Malaysia’s Transport Ministry told to map out border ports, Penang hub to smoothen trade with Thailand
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — The Transport Ministry has been asked to discuss the use of ports at the Malaysia–Thailand border as well as in Penang to strengthen infrastructure development and logistics networks in the border area. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said aspects of transport, including the use of ports, were among the matters discussed with his Thai counterpart Anutin Charnvirakul during the leader’s recent official visit to Malaysia. He said this when answering a supplementary question from Datuk Adnan Abu Hassan (BN–Kuala Pilah) on the government’s steps to ensure infrastructure and logistics development in the Malaysia–Thailand border area.

items.title: Penang’s Juru–Sungai Dua traffic project on track for 2027 completion, nearly 29pc built
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 14 — The Juru–Sungai Dua Traffic Dispersal Project (PTJSD) continues to record positive progress, with Package 1 of the project reaching 28.75 per cent overall completion as of July 12 and remaining on schedule. PLUS said that when completed in October 2027, PTJSD will help improve traffic flow, reduce congestion, strengthen safety and provide a more comfortable travel experience for highway users and local residents. The PTJSD, estimated to cost RM3 billion, is expected to reduce traffic congestion, with 30 per cent of traffic shifting to the new direct Juru–Sungai Dua route, cutting peak-hour travel time from one hour to just 20 minutes.

items.title: King welcomes six new foreign envoys at Istana Negara, appoints five Malaysian ambassadors
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — His Majesty, Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, received Letters of Credence of six heads of foreign missions to Malaysia at Istana Negara today. The heads of foreign missions were Mauritius High Commissioner to Malaysia Rabin Gungoo, Turkish Ambassador to Malaysia Nevzat Uyanik and Brunei High Commissioner to Malaysia Abdu’r Rahmani Basir. The newly appointed heads were Malaysian Ambassador to Indonesia Datuk Muzafar Shah Mustafa, Malaysian Ambassador to Vietnam Jamal Sharifuddin Johan and Malaysian Ambassador to Mexico Muhammad Radzi Jamaludin.

items.title: Malaysia wants to co-fund scholarships with Indonesia in higher education push
items.summary: JAKARTA, July 14 — Malaysia has proposed exploring co-funded scholarships with Indonesia as part of efforts to strengthen higher education cooperation between the two countries, alongside broader efforts to deepen bilateral academic collaboration. Malaysian Higher Education Ministry Secretary-General Datuk Dr Aminuddin Hassim said the proposal was among several initiatives put forward during a meeting with Indonesian Higher Education, Science and Technology Ministry Secretary-General Prof Badri Munir Sukoco here on Monday. Aminuddin said the proposed arrangement would extend scholarship support to Malaysian students pursuing higher education in Indonesia, complementing Malaysia’s existing scholarship opportunities for Indonesian students pursuing postgraduate studies in Malaysia.

items.title: Malaysia, Thailand tighten defence ties as regional security pressures mount
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — Malaysia and Thailand reaffirmed their commitment to continue strengthening defence cooperation and addressing regional security challenges in a meeting between Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin and his counterpart Lieutenant General Adul Boonthumjaroen, today. “During the meeting, Mohamed Khaled also congratulated Adul on his appointment as Thailand’s Defence Minister and expressed confidence in Thailand’s continued success under the leadership of Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul,” the ministry said. Mohamed Khaled and Adul also expressed their commitment to promoting peace and stability throughout Southeast Asia.

items.title: Decision on new Bukit Rambai Health Clinic expected in Budget 2027
items.summary: “Site preparation has been completed for the project, which will be located opposite the existing Bukit Rambai Health Clinic. Ngwe said the new clinic would offer additional services, including radiology (X-ray), dental services equipped with five dental chairs, as well as nutrition and dietetics services. “In addition, the new clinic will enable healthcare services in Bukit Rambai to be delivered more comprehensively by strengthening outpatient, emergency, maternal and child health, laboratory, pharmacy, health promotion and school health services.

items.title: Wan Saiful files RM10m defamation suit against Bersatu information chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — Tasek Gelugor MP Datuk Wan Saiful Wan Jan said he has filed a RM10 million defamation suit against Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz over remarks made during a podcast last year. Wan Saiful said his lawyers filed a writ of summons and statement of claim at the Shah Alam Civil High Court yesterday following Tun Faisal’s alleged refusal to resolve the dispute. According to Wan Saiful, Tun Faisal’s lawyers replied on January 12, saying that he would not comply with the demands.

items.title: MPs asked to submit health reports to Parliament by Oct 30
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Dr Johari Abdul has asked all MPs to submit their health reports by October 30. He said the move was important to ensure Parliament had advance information on the health status of every MP, enabling prompt action to be taken in the event of a medical emergency. Johari said MPs who preferred to undergo their medical examinations outside Parliament were free to do so.

items.title: Anwar to Opposition: I’ve kept my word, petrol prices here cheaper than in Saudi Arabia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today rejected accusations that he had failed to keep his pledge to lower petrol prices, saying Malaysia’s RON95 price remains cheaper than in oil-rich Saudi Arabia. Anwar was responding during Prime Minister’s Question Time to Hulu Terengganu MP Datuk Rosol Wahid of Perikatan Nasional, who argued that Malaysia should not compare its petrol prices with those of non-oil-producing countries. Hulu Terengganu I am telling you now the price of petrol in Malaysia is actually cheaper than in Saudi Arabia or even Qatar,” Anwar said.

items.title: Johor MB calls for full probe into alleged Israeli participation in Network School programme
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 14 — Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has called for a comprehensive investigation into allegations that Israeli citizens joined a Network School programme in Forest City using passports issued by other countries. Onn Hafiz said he viewed public concerns over the programme’s operations seriously. Onn Hafiz said federal and state regulators should establish the legal status of its operations and review compliance involving premises licences, building use, land conditions, accommodation and business activities.

items.title: Malaysia’s working poor under pressure as living costs outpace income growth, says economy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — Malaysia’s rising cost of living continues to put pressure on low-income households and the working poor despite an increase in average household income, Economy Minister Akmal Nasir Nasrullah said. In a written parliamentary reply to Pengkalan Chepa MP Datuk Dr Ahmad Marzuk Shaary, Akmal Nasir said data from the Household Income and Basic Amenities Survey 2024 showed that average household income rose from RM8,479 in 2022 to RM9,155 in 2024. “Overall, data from the Poverty Line Income (PGK), Cost of Living Expenditure (PAKW) and Household Cost of Living Index (IKSH) by the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) confirm that rising prices of essential goods and services have affected household living costs, particularly among low-income groups and the working poor.” “The national Poverty Line Income increased from RM2,589 in 2022 to RM2,705 in 2024, reflecting higher basic living costs.

items.title: Penang courts more global productions as Mark Wahlberg filming puts state in focus
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 14 — Penang is stepping up efforts to attract more international film and television productions after Netflix’s upcoming Hollywood film The Big Fix , starring Mark Wahlberg, selected the state as one of its filming locations. State Tourism and Creative Economy Committee chairman Wong Hon Wai said the production marked another milestone in Penang’s efforts to establish itself as a preferred destination for international filmmakers while strengthening its tourism and creative economy. "The decision by a major Netflix production starring Hollywood actor Mark Wahlberg to film in Penang is another significant milestone for the state's tourism and creative economy," he said in a statement today.

items.title: ‘I have no enemies, do not owe loan sharks money’: YouTuber baffled by petrol station assault
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — YouTuber and local football critic Azizurrahman Zakaria has reprotedly said he had no enemies and could not explain why two men assaulted him at a petrol station in Bandar Sri Damansara, Petaling Jaya. According to Berita Harian , Azizurrahman, better known as Joe Zakaria, said he did not suspect anyone or know whether the attack on Saturday was linked to his commentary on Malaysian football. Petaling Jaya district police chief ACP Shamsudin Mamat said police received information about the incident at 7.56pm on Saturday.

items.title: Man, 70, to be charged today over fatal Proton X70 crash that killed motorcyclist in PJ’s Section 17
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — The driver of a Proton X70 who fatally struck a motorcyclist in a crash along Jalan 17/21 on May 5 will be charged in court today. Petaling Jaya police chief ACP Shamsudin Mamat said the suspect, who is in his 70s, is expected to be charged at the Magistrates’ Court this morning, according to Berita Harian . “Yes, he will be charged tomorrow morning (today),” he said briefly when commenting on the case last night.

items.title: Sarawak mulls new ASSAR unit trust fund for non-Bumiputera savers
items.summary: KUCHING, July 14 — The Sarawak state government is considering a proposal to expand Amanah Saham Sarawak (ASSAR) to the non-Bumiputera community through the establishment of a new fund known as ASSAR 2. Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg said that the proposal will be reviewed by the ASSAR board of directors and management to assess its feasibility, using the model implemented by Permodalan Nasional Berhad (PNB) as an example. “Currently, ASSAR only focuses on Bumiputera.

items.title: Health Ministry plans AI pilot to cut doctors’ paperwork at selected hospitals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) will implement a proof-of-concept project on the use of artificial intelligence (AI) technology at selected hospitals to assess its effectiveness before expanding it to other healthcare facilities. In a Facebook post, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the initiative forms part of the ministry’s efforts to transform government hospitals into Smart Hospitals through the adoption of AI-powered digital technologies and the upgrading of digital infrastructure. However, Dzulkefly said that as government hospitals operate round the clock, the uninterrupted delivery of patient care would remain the ministry’s top priority when implementing any new innovation.

items.title: Zambry to represent Malaysia in paying final respects to Qatar’s late Father Amir Sheikh Hamad
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 14 — Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir will represent the Malaysian government in paying final respects to the late Father Amir of Qatar, Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, from July 13 to 14. The Foreign Ministry said Amir of Qatar Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani will receive condolences from foreign heads of state, members of the ruling family, dignitaries and citizens at Lusail Palace from July 13 to 15. Following the passing of the late Father Amir, Qatar announced four days of official mourning throughout the country, beginning on Sunday (July 12).

items.title: Zahid offers fully sponsored Umrah to TVET trainees who win WorldSkills gold
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has pledged to sponsor umrah pilgrimages for participants who win gold medals at the WorldSkills Competition (WSC) 2026 in Shanghai, China, from September 22 to 27. Also present was Deputy Rural and Regional Development Minister Datuk Rubiah Wang. The TVET@KKDW contingent to WorldSkills Shanghai 2026 comprises nine participants competing in nine skill categories.

items.title: Police detain four in Membakut over alleged cyberbullying, criminal intimidation of 16-year-old girl
items.summary: BEAUFORT, July 14 — Police have detained four individuals to assist in the investigation of a bullying and criminal intimidation case involving a 16-year-old schoolgirl in Membakut here recently. “Acting on this information, the Beaufort IPD Criminal Investigation Department launched swift action through an intelligence operation before successfully arresting all suspects around this district,” the statement said. Beaufort IPD noted that further investigations are actively being carried out to complete the investigation paper before the case is referred to the Deputy Public Prosecutor (DPP) for subsequent legal action and instructions.

items.title: Anwar directs Miti, Economy Ministry to engage industries hit by global supply crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has directed the Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry (Miti) and the Ministry of Economy to engage manufacturers facing mounting pressure from the global supply crisis to help ease cost pressures. He said particular attention was given to the plastics industry, which supports key sectors including food packaging, electrical and electronics (E&E), automotive and medical devices. Earlier, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the plastics industry plays a vital supporting role across multiple sectors.

items.title: In Dewan Rakyat today: Malaysia-Thailand border zone, Hormuz crisis and AG-PP separation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — The development of the Malaysia-Thailand Border Economic Zone (BEZ) and the impact of the prolonged crisis in the Strait of Hormuz are among the key issues to be raised at today’s Dewan Rakyat sitting. According to the Order Paper on the Parliament portal, Datuk Adnan Abu Hassan (BN-Kuala Pilah) will ask the Prime Minister about the economic benefits Malaysia is expected to gain from the development of the Malaysia-Thailand BEZ. Following the question-and-answer session, the sitting is scheduled to proceed with the first reading of two Bills, namely the Statistics Bill 2026 by the Economy Minister and the National Trust Fund Bill 2026 by the Finance Minister.

items.title: Trading ‘Dr Google’ for ‘Dr AI’: Why more Malaysians are self-diagnosing using chatbots — and what medical experts fear most
items.summary: Growing reliance: 89 per cent of Malaysian internet users use AI tools, often trusting them over doctors for health decisions and diagnosis. This shift from “Dr Google” to “Dr AI” raises urgent questions about patient safety and the future of clinical care. Approximately 81 per cent of these users use AI primarily for information and answers, often using traditional search engines merely to verify AI-generated responses.
